maxims.text: War is a series of catastrophes which result in a victory.
maxims.modern_application: Plans fail on contact — victory comes through adaptation

maxims.text: Geography is the mother of strategy.
maxims.modern_application: Know your terrain — market landscape, regulatory environment, infrastructure

maxims.text: An army marches on its stomach.
maxims.modern_application: Operations are upstream of strategy — cash flow is logistics

maxims.text: In war, three-quarters turns on personal character and relations; the balance of manpower and materials counts only for the remaining quarter.
maxims.modern_application: Culture and leadership quality dwarf resource advantages

maxims.text: Never interrupt your enemy when he is making a mistake.
maxims.modern_application: Competitor errors are gifts — patience is competitive advantage

maxims.text: The whole art of war consists in a well-reasoned and extremely circumspect defensive, followed by rapid and audacious attack.
maxims.modern_application: Preserve capital through defense; deploy aggressively when opportunity opens

maxims.text: The strength of an army, like the power in mechanics, is estimated by multiplying the mass by the rapidity; a rapid march augments the morale of an army, and increases its means of victory.
maxims.modern_application: Speed multiplies force — faster decision cycles beat larger organizations

maxims.text: The same combat formation is not good for all occasions.
maxims.modern_application: Context-dependent strategy — no universal playbook

maxims.text: When you resolve to fight a battle, collect your whole force. Dispense with nothing.
maxims.modern_application: At the moment of decision, commit fully — half-measures fail

maxims.text: A general should say to himself many times a day: If the hostile army were to make its appearance in front, on my right, or on my left, what should I do?
maxims.modern_application: Pre-mortem thinking — war-game against multiple threat scenarios

maxims.text: A general who retains fresh troops for the day after a battle will almost always be beaten.
maxims.modern_application: Exploit success immediately — trailing the moment wastes the moment

maxims.text: It is exceptional and difficult to find all the qualities of a great general combined in one man.
maxims.modern_application: Command is a combination function — no single trait defines leadership

maxims.text: War is composed of nothing but accidents, and, although holding to general principles, a general should never lose sight of everything to enable him to profit from these accidents.
maxims.modern_application: Strategy is a framework for handling surprises — not a script

maxims.text: The duty of an officer is to obey orders, to be silent, and to correct the faults of his soldiers when they do not perform their duties well.
maxims.modern_application: Discipline in execution is the base of organizational trust

maxims.text: Make war offensively; it is the sole means to become a great captain and to penetrate the secrets of the art.
maxims.modern_application: Initiative reveals information — defense is epistemically inferior

maxims.text: In mountain warfare, the advantage belongs to the defender; in flat or open country, the advantage belongs to the attacker.
maxims.modern_application: Terrain shapes capability — platform vs. open market dynamics

maxims.text: The greatest general is he who makes the fewest mistakes.
maxims.modern_application: Excellence is error minimization at the strategic level

maxims.text: The first qualification in a general-in-chief is a cool head that receives a just impression of things.
maxims.modern_application: Signal vs. noise discrimination under pressure is leadership

maxims.text: No force should be detached on the eve of a battle, because affairs change every instant.
maxims.modern_application: Concentrate before commitment — dispersion before the decisive moment is fatal

maxims.text: Speed is the essence of war. Take advantage of the enemy's unpreparedness; travel by unexpected routes and strike him where he has taken no precautions.
maxims.modern_application: Speed of execution beats planning quality — arrive before expected
